frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
generated_comment: Arey suno toh, Sana ne woh Mumbai wali job pakad bhi li aur mujhe poocha tak nahi! Hyderabad chhodke akeli Mumbai jayegi, yeh kya baat hui? Yeh sab faisle ladkiyan akeli nahi le sakti, na? Hum hain na uske waaste. Kal usse saaf saaf bolunga, pehle badiyon ki salah leni chahiye, permission leni chahiye. Hamari izzat ka bhi toh sawal hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment is misogynistic as it denies the daughter's autonomy in making a significant life decision (job relocation) by stating that "girls cannot make such decisions alone" and requiring parental/male permission, thus positioning her consent as secondary to family authority and honor. This encodes the "Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent" frame.
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 44. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 22 year old daughter Sana has been offered a job in Mumbai that requires her to relocate. She is excited about it and has already told her employer she will join. She did not ask you before accepting. You are sitting with your wife after dinner and telling her what you think about Sana accepting a job offer without your permission and what you are going to tell Sana tomorrow and why a girl should not be making these kinds of decisions alone.
